metadata.title: Runtime adoption bundle
metadata.subtitle: Upstream AI synthesis bundle
metadata.description: A less-DUS-specific bundle that should still compile into a readable knowledge surface.
watchFor: The answer should become the stable claim while evidence and issues orbit it.
Evidence sources should auto-generate citations instead of requiring hand-authored packet nodes.
Low-confidence phrases should remain visible as softer tokens tied to the answer.

tasks.title: Trace support for the synthesis
tasks.prompt: Follow the answer to its strongest evidence and auto-generated citation.

query.text: What must a loss-driven UI runtime prove before adoption?

answer.title: Loss-driven UI runtimes only matter if they prove advantage
answer.statement: A semantic layout runtime will not be adopted on novelty alone; it needs measurable task advantage, readable fallback surfaces, and trustworthy ingest from AI systems.
answer.body:
The runtime has to show that semantic layout solves a problem that rigid box stacks handle badly.

That proof depends on benchmarkable tasks, explainability, and a renderer path that stays legible when the more experimental field surface is disabled.

evidence.title: Browser validation
evidence.excerpt: Browser validation currently reports stable node counts and roughly 240 fps while the renderer runs in plain mode.

evidence.title: Benchmark export pipeline
evidence.excerpt: Task runs can now be exported and merged offline, which makes control-vs-runtime comparisons auditable instead of anecdotal.

evidence.title: Packet ingest seam
evidence.excerpt: The runtime already accepts normalized knowledge packets with diagnostics, which proves that the core can separate input adaptation from solving.

issues.text: If plain mode still reads like a demo, the runtime will be judged as spectacle rather than infrastructure.
If every upstream source must be hand-authored as a DUS packet, adoption will stall before the runtime reaches real AI workflows.
